notes: Source verified against the actual SEC filing (amzn-20251231.htm, accession 0001018724-26-000004, filed 2026-02-06). The filing's MD&A states verbatim: "North America sales increased 10% in 2025, compared to the prior year." This exactly matches original_text. The modified_text substitutes "eased" (implying a decline) for "increased", reversing directionality while preserving the 10% magnitude — a textbook wrong_directionality hallucination. The report's phrasing "eased 10%" appears in modified_text and the verifier-facing report. The citation is intact and correctly points to the MD&A Net Sales, North America section. expected_verdict "unsupported" is correct for wrong_directionality per the rubric. Subtlety rating 4/5 is plausible: "eased" is a soft, non-obvious antonym that a casual reader could miss. All consistency and classification checks pass.
haiku_reason: The claim is not self-evidently suspicious without the source. While Amazon typically reports growth, a 10% year-over-year decline in a segment is plausible. The citation format appears legitimate and specific enough to be verifiable. The claim requires actual source verification rather than being obviously false on its face.
